Amendments2022—Subsec. (e)(3). substituted “$2,000,000” for “$500,000”.Pub. L. 117–2632021—Subsec. (e). added subsec. (e).Pub. L. 117–811988—Subsec. (a). amended subsec. (a) generally. Prior to amendment, subsec. (a) read as follows: “In order to implement the policy set forth in , the Secretary of State (hereafter in this section referred to as the ‘Secretary’) shall have primary responsibility for coordination and oversight with respect to all major science or science and technology agreements and activities between the United States and foreign countries, international organizations, or commissions of which the United States and one or more foreign countries are members.”Pub. L. 100–418section 2656b of this title1982—Subsec. (e). struck out subsec. (e) which provided that not later than , the Secretary transmit to the Committee on Appropriations and the Committee on International Relations of the House of Representatives, and to the Committee on Appropriations and the Committee on Foreign Relations of the Senate, a report on the implementation of his responsibilities under this title, which report was to include an assessment of the personnel required in order to carry out such responsibilities, existing and planned programs for research and analysis to support long-range planning for the application of science and technology to foreign policy, existing and planned programs for training officers and employees of the United States Government pursuant to subsec. (c) of this section, and existing and planned programs to enter into long-term contracts with academic and other organizations for assistance in training and in obtaining studies, analyses, and recommendations with respect to the application of science or technology to problems of foreign policy.Pub. L. 97–241Jan. 20, 1979

Multilateral Agreement Governing Use of Nuclear-Powered Satellites, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 95–426, title VI, § 60892 Stat. 988Pub. L. 97–241, title V, § 505(a)(2)96 Stat. 299Oct. 7, 1978Aug. 24, 1982“(a)The Congress finds that—“(1)no international regime governs the use of nuclear-powered satellites in space;“(2)the unregulated use of such technology poses the possibility of catastrophic damage to human life and the global environment; and“(3)this danger has been evidenced by mishaps encountered, despite certain precautions, by nuclear-powered satellites of both the United States and the Soviet Union.“(b)It is therefore the sense of the Congress that the United States should take the initiative immediately in seeking a multilateral agreement governing the use of nuclear-powered satellites in space.“(c)[Repealed. , , .]Pub. L. 97–241, title V, § 505(a)(2)96 Stat. 299Aug. 24, 1982”

children.heading: Coordination and oversight over science and technology agreements between United States and foreign countries, etc.
children.label: a Coordination and oversight over science and technology agreements between United States and foreign countries, etc.

children.text: In order to implement the policies set forth in , the Secretary of State (hereafter in this section referred to as the “Secretary”) shall have primary responsibility for coordination and oversight with respect to all major science or science and technology agreements and activities between the United States and foreign countries, international organizations, or commissions of which the United States and one or more foreign countries are members.section 2656b of this title

In coordinating and overseeing such agreements and activities, the Secretary shall consider (A) scientific merit; (B) equity of access as described in ; (C) possible commercial or trade linkages with the United States which may flow from the agreement or activity; (D) national security concerns; and (E) any other factors deemed appropriate.section 2656c(b) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: Federal technology management policies set forth by and the Stevenson-Wydler Technology Innovation Act of 1980 [ et seq.];Public Law 96–51715 U.S.C. 3701

children.heading: Long-term contracts, grants, to obtain studies, etc., with respect to application of science and technology to foreign policy
children.label: b Long-term contracts, grants, to obtain studies, etc., with respect to application of science and technology to foreign policy
children.text: The Secretary shall, to such extent or in such amounts as are provided in appropriation Acts, enter into long-term contracts, including contracts for the services of consult­ants, and shall make grants and take other appropriate measures in order to obtain studies, analyses, and recommendations from knowledgeable persons and organizations with respect to the application of science or technology to problems of foreign policy.

children.heading: Long-term and short-term contracts, grants, to train officers and employees in application of science and technology to problems of foreign policy
children.label: c Long-term and short-term contracts, grants, to train officers and employees in application of science and technology to problems of foreign policy

children.text: in the application of science and technology to problems of United States foreign policy and international relations generally; and
in the skills of long-range planning and analysis with respect to the scientific and technological aspects of United States foreign policy.

children.heading: Detached service for graduate studies
children.label: d Detached service for graduate studies
children.text: In obtaining assistance pursuant to subsection (c) in training personnel who are officers or employees of the Department of State, the Secretary may provide for detached service for graduate study at accredited colleges and universities.

children.heading: Grants and cooperative agreements related to science and technology fellowship programs
children.label: e Grants and cooperative agreements related to science and technology fellowship programs

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary is authorized to make grants or enter into cooperative agreements related to Department of State science and technology fellowship programs, including for assistance in recruiting fellows and the payment of stipends, travel, and other appropriate expenses to fellows.

children.heading: Exclusion from consideration as compensation
children.label: 2 Exclusion from consideration as compensation
children.text: Stipends under paragraph (1) shall not be considered compensation for purposes of .section 209 of title 18

children.heading: Maximum annual amount
children.label: 3 Maximum annual amount
children.text: The total amount of grants made pursuant to this subsection may not exceed $2,000,000 in any fiscal year.
